question.text: A call option has a strike price of Rs. 500 and the underlying stock is currently trading at Rs. 550. This option is:

answer.text: In-the-Money (ITM) with intrinsic value of Rs. 50
In-the-Money (ITM) with intrinsic value of Rs. 500

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A call option is ITM when the underlying price (Rs. 550) > strike price (Rs. 500). The intrinsic value = Current price - Strike price = Rs. 550 - Rs. 500 = Rs. 50. The holder can exercise to buy at Rs. 500 and immediately sell at Rs. 550, gaining Rs. 50. **The Trap:** Rs. 500 is the strike price, not the intrinsic value. Intrinsic value is always the favorable difference between market price and strike price. **Cross-Exam Context:** Option moneyness (ITM/ATM/OTM) and intrinsic value are core concepts in VIII and X-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** ITM options have both intrinsic value and time value; OTM options have only time value. As expiry approaches, time value decays — advise clients to be aware of time decay when holding options.
